response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished full-stack/web software engineer with strong TypeScript, Node.js, and some low-latency systems experience (DLang at Dunnhumby), but his background is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position requires SoC and IP modeling, AI accelerator performance analysis, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in his experience. His hardware exposure is limited to brief C/C++ IDE component work at National Instruments and hobby-level embedded projects, and there is no deep-learning workload or computer-architecture experience at all.
strengths: 15+ years of software engineering experience, satisfying the BS + 8 years experience path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems work (async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang at millions of requests/sec)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and teaching/mentoring track record
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon design feedback, or hardware performance-modeling experience
No AI/deep-learning workload, GPU architecture, or SIMD/MIMD accelerator background
Career trajectory is web/product engineering, not the principal-level computer-architecture depth this role demands

key_factors.factor: No deep-learning or GPU workload experience
